name: Thinking Space II
description: An official sequel to Thinking Space, and currently the hardest rated level in the game. Unlike the original, its decoration is in a glow design style, but it's more similar in gameplay as it has several gameplay references to the original and is also extremely end carried. The first top 1 in many years that was not built intentionally to be top 1.

name: Flamewall
description: Almost seven minutes of extremely fast and challenging gameplay, using a similarly very fast and dramatic symphonic metal song, and featuring very few break sections for its length. Features a gargantuan roster of creators and took multiple years to come out, in part thanks to the verifier dying at 99% twice.

name: Amethyst
description: A former top 1, dethroning Tidal Wave after two years. It's inspired by the Golden, with cave decoration with rocks, geodes, and purple and pink crystals. The gameplay consists of a lot of extremely hard wave and ship gameplay.

name: Tidal Wave
description: A former Top 1 and Onilink's first rated level, featuring incredibly difficult wave sections spread over its 3 minute runtime. Unlike most other top 1s, this level takes a very different approach to its style, theming it around the ocean and the beach with its blue and yellow colour scheme.

name: ORBIT
description: Another satisfying MindCap project tackling the theme of orbs. According to MindCap, this is the toughest project he and his roster have ever worked on with multiple creators dropping the level. Don't fall into The Ball Pit™.

name: Nullscapes
description: The sequel to Shardscapes and the current hardest level to use one gamemode, that being robot. Expanding on its predecessor, it features more noticeable progression, an orb cycling gimmick and most importantly much more unforgiving gameplay. Includes a CBF blocker which has sparked a significant amount of controversy within the community.

name: Quanteuse processing
description: An uncanny and extremely raw superbuffed remake of the main level Blast Processing, with gameplay by Renn241 and Blankb. Not meant to be a serious level.

name: BOOBAWAMBA
description: The current hardest memory extreme, themed around a dark abandoned factory with limited visibility. Features a randomized ending where five details throughout the level determine whether or not to click their corresponding toggle triggers. Built over the course of two years and thousands of hours, Akunakun considers this his magnum opus.

name: Every End
description: The hardest endurance level in Geometry Dash, featuring over 7 minutes of simple yet brutal 1.0 cube timings. Its difficulty balancing and specific skillset have lead it to being very accessible for a top level. Despite its simple 1.0 aesthetic, its technically impressive background effect involved the usage of third party software developed by Absolute.

name: Silent clubstep
description: One of the most legendary and recognizable levels in Geometry Dash. Formerly impossible and was initially hack-verified by Sailent in 1.8. Houses the "8 Jumps of Hell" and the infamous 6% ship part, among other classic sections.

name: andromeda
description: Relatively unknown considering it's difficulty. Pink and purple space themed level with incredibly high-CPS gameplay and flashy visuals. Took over a year to be completed.

name: Subsuming Vortex
description: The hardest nine circles level currently on the list. It was created all the way in 2018 by SeptaGon7 and redecorated by Renn241. The name is a reference to a Calamity Mod spell tome with the same name.

name: Anathema
description: Legendary finale of the Dedication Series that took over 6 years to be finished and verified. Reminiscent of many different styles throughout 2.1.

name: Ashley Wave Trials
description: A level with a pink/red color scheme that is entirely wave, as the name suggests. The level remained unrated until CBF was added to the game and the level was eventually rated a while later.

name: Avernus
description: Well-known, poorly received, but unexpectedly enjoyable hell themed extreme which has gone through its fair share of controversy. Recognizable for its fixed hitboxes and ridiculous ship and wave sections.

name: Acheron
description: Iconic sequel to Tartarus. Intimidating 1.9 styled level that gradually gets less red and more black as it continues. Known for its impossibly tight wave sections & chokepoints.

name: Spectre
description: Zodiac sequel that was in the works for 4 years. Vaguely cool-toned with many parts by some of the most famous creators, like CuLuC, Darwin and Helpegasus.

name: Menace
description: White, orange and black effect level with plenty of lore and an entire poem within it. Includes some incredibly unorthodox gameplay. The level's song was also created by the creator, Arraegen, specifically for this project.

name: Abyss of Darkness
description: Iconic megacollab with an equally as iconic song. Originally was finished in 2018, but was redecorated in 2021. Known for its slow gameplay and very inconsistent decoration quality.

name: Defeated Circles
description: A Nine Circles styled level which takes heavy inspiration from the "You So Defeated" challenges, being especially apparent in the level's timing-based segments and its song choice. The level is overall first-half heavy, though the difficulty does not step down much in the latter half, ending off on a dual wave and a spam corridor.

name: Tunnel of Despair
description: Timing based greyscale level with red as its secondary color. Decorated exclusively by ivyteal. Generally repetitive with poorly received gameplay. Verification also features Zoink admitting to "hacking" Eternal Moment.

name: Kyouki
description: Blue and purple glowy effect level and Demishio's 3rd extreme demon project. Memorably high quality gameplay and effects with an iconic Creo song.

name: Based After Based
description: Initially a top 10 made without spikes and entirely dual gameplay that avoids speed changes. The verifier somehow fluked the level from 78%.

name: Subterminal Point
description: Generally underwhelming Trueffet sequel with classic SyQual visuals. Has an incredibly low enjoyment rating with appalling chokepoints and hitboxes, paired with a bubbly song.

name: Slaughterhouse
description: Former top 1 with some of the most iconic wave sections in the game. Memorable for how incredibly intimidating the gameplay is, paired with an extremely washed out menacing song. Nowadays its been able to be completed 3 times in a row.

name: KOCMOC
description: CherryTeam's most difficult level on the list. Although the level is mostly flashy pink and purple, it takes heavy inspiration from Slaughterhouse and mostly consists of tight wave spam and straight-fly. Pronounced as "cosmos".

name: The Lightning Rod
description: Distinct and incredibly creative extreme themed around traversing and escaping the inside of an electric tower/rod. Supposedly one of the learniest levels on the main-list with extremely high-cps and quirky gameplay.

name: CHIL
description: A pure solo project which started out in early 2.2 and took over a year to verify. To fit its Camellia song, the visuals are extremely trippy with relentless camera effect usage and rapid pulses, which are especially apparent in the second half.

name: Sakupen Circles
description: Flashy nine circles level known for its lack of balancing and iconic wave sections. Remake of the 1.9 impossible level of the same name, and was briefly top 1.

name: Deimos (ItsHybrid)
description: Extremely large megacollab and remake of Phobos. Parts significantly vary in design and color similarly to Phobos, and the iconic final dual was kept nearly identical.

name: Eyes in the Water
description: Well known for its unique decoration, unique "eye" theme, and iconic song. Includes a very strange cast of creators, with Bli, ivyteal, and skywalker14 all participating. First ship is incredibly difficult.

name: Voltage (ThePurgatory)
description: A yellow/orange electricity themed level created by The Purgatory team that took roughly four years to finish. Strangely it is pretty underground for its difficulty.

name: KOSETSU
description: Strange wintery realism collab with incredibly talented creators like Bli & Motheye. Uses an incredibly strange atmospheric song and goes a little crazy with 2.2 shaders.

name: Through The Gates
description: An initially hacked-verified level in 2018 that went through several stages. Even though it was verified in 2023, it took another three years for the level to get rated alongside other historical levels. Some say this level is a spiritual successor to Thinking Space.

name: Firework
description: Iconic greyscale former top 1 by CherryTeam. Loaded with classic sections like the red-orb ship and the difficult wave burst at 49%. Surprisingly not wave and ship carried.

name: poocubed
description: The 3rd level in the poocu series, consisting of some of the most colorful and innocent decoration on the list. Known for its high-cps off-the-grid gameplay, as well as overall maximum bubbliness.

name: CONVULSION
description: The hardest 1.9GDPS level, taking on a red, black and blue colour scheme with very flashy visuals and a menacing atmosphere. Its gameplay is very learny with many fakes and structures blending in with the decoration, and features a difficult ship section halfway through.

name: MINUSdry
description: Half-joke and half-serious old styled extreme. Remake of the impossible level "Silent Dry". Don't get wet...

name: Saul Goodman
description: The hardest extreme in the Breaking Bad series, overall not supposed to be a serious level. Incredibly learny gameplay where everything is moving and bouncing, also filled to the brim with inside jokes. Atrocious endscreen.

name: Apocalyptic Trilogy
description: Legendary but poorly received extreme that combines and superbuffs Cataclysm, Bloodbath and Aftermath. Took roughly 5 years to be finished and verified and has incredibly rough quality inconsistency.

name: Sevvend Clubstep
description: Industrial, minimalist cherry team level that's pretty click-sync.

name: The Hallucination
description: Black and blue circles level known for its intense song and fast-paced sync gameplay. Decoration took over a year to be completed.

name: COMBUSTION
description: Claustrophobic 1.9 styled extreme with only about 50 seconds of gameplay. Created to be one of the hardest slow, timing based extremes at the time.

name: Deadlier Clubstep
description: Maximalist authentic 1.9 level with some of the densest decoration on the list. Vague remake of Deadly Clubstep and Silent Clubstep, mimicking the classic cube-ball dual. Has its fair share of edgy text sprinkled throughout.

name: Edge of Destiny
description: Beloved remake of classic extreme Blade of Justice. Stays very true to the original, but significantly enhances the decoration and buffs the gameplay into a bright and glowy masterpiece.

name: Solar Flare
description: A remake of Solar Circles, with warm colours and blue highlights. Consists of unique structuring and a lot of movements on the structures. Also features Janet, the sun at the halfway point.

name: The Catacombs
description: Very old 1.9 GDPS level built as an upcoming top 1 is 2019. Was verified by Slithium in 2024, and rated in 2026.

name: LIMBO
description: Legendary memory level hosted by MindCap. Built with the intention of providing a unique, different experience to players. The randomised key maze is perhaps most iconic ending in any extreme. One of the first extremes to have merch.

name: Gaggatrondra
description: Bright and energetic level using Problem by Ariana Grande. Includes a memorable dual with intense and quirky high-cps gameplay. With the level containing a sign pointing to the "Zunty Realm", this extreme clearly is not meant to be taken too seriously. The most enjoyed level in the top 150 according to EDEL.

name: Belladonna
description: Gameplay by Vernam, effects and structuring by Itami. Memorable for its flashy visuals and high-cps flow gameplay.

name: Codependence (Solo)
description: 2-player megacollab released in 2020 by TCTeam. It took roughly 3 years after the level's release for it to be verified solo. Aged surprisingly well.

name: Collapse (Nexel)
description: Colorful XL click-sync solo design level using the second half of Dimrain47's Infernoplex. Memorable for its flashy rainbow effects and song representation. Contains an entire 10 second dash-orb at 76% to optimize the amount of nerves. 🐟

name: Mayhem
description: Construction themed CherryTeam collab mostly memorable for its off-sync sync-based gameplay. The LDM is perhaps a little too strong...

name: The Plunge
description: Atmospheric and floaty vortex themed extreme with a washed out song that takes loose White Space inspiration. Very memorable endscreen with a realistic hand.

name: walter white
description: The 2nd hardest level in the Breaking Bad series, and arguably the least interesting. Consists of typical Renn241 styled decoration with great colours and a severe lack of move triggers.

name: Operation Evolution
description: The third hardest 1.9 GDPS level which features 5 minutes of memory gameplay!

name: Damascus
description: Incredibly large megacollab 3 years in the making in which many classic creators (such as Tygrysek and Rob Buck) would remake their own most important level to them as their part. A level serving as a collage of diverse creating styles and scattered memories over the years, and was fittingly released on Geometry Dash's 10 year anniversary.

name: SARYYX NEVER CLEAR
description: SARY NEVER CLEAR and Hard Machine inspired design extreme with bright colors and heavy 1.9 influence. Gameplay is also loosely inspired by the aforementioned levels, with tight straight-fly and multiple brief spam sections.

name: The Yangire
description: Well-received sequel to The Yandere. Inconsistent but incredible art and designs with parts almost 3 years old before being finished. Took over 130,000 attempts to be verified.

name: Climax
description: Typical purple glow-flow extreme heavily inspired by Kyouki, using the same song.

name: ORDINARY
description: Bright and flashy early 2.2 extreme with a classic The Living Tombstone song that gets awkwardly pitched down at 59%. Generally very poorly received by victors. forgive no run please stop die haha

name: Loops of Fury
description: Grayscale effect level that contains sections where the gameplay loops several times over (hence its name). Has a very chokeable ending.

name: Sinister Silence
description: 1.9 style extreme memorable for its nearly 9 minute runtime and incredibly large catalogue of creators. Utilizes a high-energy progressive rock song that easily gets your hands shaking as you progress.

name: Midnight
description: An extremely flashy, fast-paced and high cps sequel to Twilight. Consists of a vague space theme and brief misaligned dual section. Supposedly a collage of Yuko's older levels.

name: Cimmerian Shade
description: Extremely early 2.1 former impossible level that got rated completely out of the blue. Consists of unusual gameplay and a completely invisible section at 90%.

name: PSYCHOPATH (DeniPol)
description: Extremely unique extreme with a vague asylum and hallucination theme. The level's concept has supposedly been floating around since 2019. Caused controversy by being unrated after the verification was found to be using click between frames.

name: arcturus
description: Dark hell-themed level with a song straight out of a horror movie. Recognizable for its iconic fast-paced wave sections and 16% of the level being a black screen. Formerly an impossible level, and was part of the triple top 1 rate alongside Slaughterhouse and Firework.

name: BEELINE (Solo)
description: Very vibrant splitscreen 2 player level with the parts being references to various games. Includes some tricky and fast-paced gameplay, with the beginning being the hardest one.

name: Sonic Wave Infinity
description: Superbuffed Sonic Wave remake hosted by APTeam and Riot. 5 verifiers were involved and the level was briefly unrated because of its infamous 69% crash trigger that could corrupt save data. Supposedly, Riot hosted this megacollab because he felt the original Sonic Wave was "boring".

name: Tartarus
description: Infamous 1.9 styled megacollab with a long history of verifiers, a revamp, and controversies. A remake of the impossible level Silent Akbar. Reigned as an overwhelmingly intimidating top 1 for more than 17 months.

name: Coalescence
description: Fast-paced and intriguing fusion of extremes Fog and Shimmer. Consists of an entire minute of 4x speed gameplay. The song is also a collab by the artists involved in the two levels combined here. Features a randomised ending that is much more complex than the one found in Fog.

name: Waterfall
description: Legendary long awaited high-octane icy megacollab by CherryTeam. Known for its intense song and extremely high-effort and realistic art created by the likes of Bli, CuLuc and kivvvi. Originally was meant to be a Firework sequel.

name: Jigsaw
description: Learny high-cps level themed around the Saw movie series. Features a stacked cast of realism and effect creators with an overwhelming amount of sharp objects and edgy text throughout. Particularly memorable for it's clean transitions and background effects.

name: Delta
description: Intensely colorful and very sleek design megacollab hosted by Drakosa and Artsiburn. High-cps spinoff of the greek letter series (Gamma, Sigma & Omega) and memorable for its flashy effects and incredible colors. Also features a very satisfying drop-down out of the section at 67% into a new one to the song's key change.

name: BPATA MPAKA
description: A 2.2 Russian Mega-collab originally made to be a top 1, which was later changed. The level is based on the novel Master and Margarita.

name: Natural Disaster
description: Formerly impossible flashy green extreme with a vague nature/jungle theme. Features incredibly fast-paced wave sections and fast-paced gameplay. Stolen assets were found in the original version of the level, leading to the level getting unrated and getting a redecoration. Respect the Planet.

name: Terminal Rampancy
description: Xyriak's magnum opus that was worked on for over 3 years. It is extremely frantic with its gameplay and consists complicated usage of the dual mode, making it difficult to approach and learn. Unfortunately does not use the robot game mode.

name: The Golden
description: Legendary green and yellow jungle themed extreme that reigned as top 1 for over 100 days. Known for its extremely difficult wave sections and dark atmosphere. A remake of LMAnubis' El Dorado that very nearly didn't exist. I verified the golden lets go

name: Oblivion
description: Claustrophobic and colourful sequel to the equally claustrophobic Cognition. Loses the old fashioned style song for a more upbeat Creo song, similar to Carcano. Surprisingly one of the most dropped extreme verifications, going through 6 verifiers.

name: Viprin UFO
description: Retro 1.9 styled extreme that only uses the UFO gamemode. Gameplay is much harder than it looks due to the unpredictable click-patterns and sketchy routes. Includes an upbeat Waterflame song and references to Pacman and Flappy Bird levels.

name: NETWORK (Agat3)
description: Fast-paced greyscale neo-design collab acting as a sequel to the easier Framework. Contains a memorably tricky spider at 55%

name: Verdant Landscape
description: A pure solo level built and verified over the course of two years. Taking heavy inspiration from Windy Landscape, it features a green colour scheme, fast-paced, flowy gameplay and mesmerising Woogi-style symmetrical duals. Highly praised amongst the community for its fun gameplay and charming visual style.

name: UNKNOWN
description: Redecoration of the 1.9 styled level of the same name. The completely greyscale decoration and unnerving song makes for a generally unsettling experience. Also one of the shorter extremes on the list, being less than 45 seconds.

name: ATOMIC CANNON Mk III
description: The third installment of the ATOMIC CANNON series and the first to include features from the 2.2 update. The level follows gameplay elements from the first level compared to the second, with the cps not being as high; however, a wave part is included instead of a ship. Similar to the first installment, the last ball is a homage to Lieb's very first solo level, "Relativo," by featuring several designs from it.

name: Shukketsu
description: Learny Kowareta inspired "art-core" level with a groovy breakbeat song and colorful desaturated visuals. Features a slight garden theme and is flooded with community inside jokes and memes. The gameplay itself is clicksync and fast paced, with many chokepoints throughout the level.

name: Checked Steam
description: A Sequel to the level "Check Steam" by Gruzy, the level is known for its absolutely brutal spam sections specifically crafted for WhizKid05 to verify. Gameplay is generally hated by the community except for two victors who loved it.

name: Critical Heat
description: Fast-paced extreme themed around escaping a burning facility. Contains a lot of scaled up animated fire objects and high octane gameplay, along with a difficult dual. The hardest PEACEFUL! level in the game.

name: WOBBLING MACHINE (Solo)
description: 2 player extreme where one player has to perform standard gameplay as the other player clicks in a repetitive pattern, matching the rhythm of the 'wobbling' technique in Smash Bros. Melee. Originally hack verified and unrated, but later nerfed and re-verified after which it took over two years to get re-rated.

name: Crystal Crusher (Solo)
description: Simplistic crystal cave themed extreme. Majority of the level features convoluted and long misaligned duals.

name: limbo but uwu ig idk
description: Really bright and blinding rainbow level with a crazy wave teleporting maze at the end of it.

name: The Paroxysm of Rage
description: Superbuffed remake of Paroxysm to the point of almost copying. Slightly upgrades the visuals, and adds an extension with some glitchy effects.

name: Aerial Gleam
description: Bright blue storm themed circles level that gradually gets more decorated and flashy as it continues. Known for spawning the "CuLuC lightning" trend and for its difficult wave sections. Official sequel to Aquatic Auroras and the third level in the Weather Trilogy with 4+ verifiers.

name: Starlit Stroll
description: Simplistic atmospheric level that gets more visually complex as it continues, eventually developing into a fast-paced psychedelic and starry swirl with a slower ending. Memorable for its few gamemodes but incredibly difficult ships, waves and swing sections.

name: Henken
description: A megacollab with an entirely Polish creator roster notable for its muted colours, timing-based gameplay and difficult wave sections near the end. Much of the difficulty also lies in the first half, and contains the occasional fixed hitbox here and there.

name: Trueffet
description: Memorable blue and pink SyQual extreme with extremely difficult ship, wave, and spam sections. Originally was top 3 (even technically being top 1) and features numerous iconic high-cps wave sections. Named after Trueffet Square from Maple Story.

name: azure blast
description: An entirely Korean megacollab with a blue colour scheme and very difficult flow gameplay. Each part is distinctive with various decoration and gameplay styles as with many Korean collabs, with chokepoints found towards the middle third of the level. The first list level to use a song from the game's built-in NCS music library.

name: Kenos
description: 2019 buffed and extended remake of Sakupen Hell. Formerly top 2 and has some terrible hixboxes and 4+ verifiers. Recieved unparalled attention at the time for the verifier npesta's insane reaction became a meme across the internet.

name: Fragile
description: Megacollab and superbuffed combination of kowareta and unrated extreme Shibireta. Many community members on Twitter were advocating for Fragile to get rated, starting the popular #RateFragile hashtag.

name: chrome hearts
description: Roadbose's sendoff to the ICE Carbon series; a core-styled level built in the 1.9GDPS with symmetrical structuring, abstract shapes and highly detailed designs. Its 2m 30s runtime consists of difficult ship and wave sections, separated in the middle by a minute-long guitar solo interlude with easier gameplay.

name: Starlight Summit
description: A pure solo extreme using only the robot gamemode where the player traverses through treacherous alpine terrain. It combines traditional pixel style decoration, editor particles and a C418 song to create a cozy atmosphere. Don't let its short length fool you, it is filled to the brim with brutal robot timings, spike corner jumps and double clicks, especially in its middle section.

name: Esfera
description: Fast-paced Korean megacollab using a primarily blue and pink color scheme. Over 4 minutes long and memorable for its inconsistent style and bubbly song, as well as its fast-paced gameplay and chokepoints.

name: NEUTRA
description: Extremely bouncy and high-energy level with incredibly flashy effects and designs. Contains a memorable dual wave at 23% along with other quirky click-sync gameplay gimmicks and a very hard ending. The music collective 'siximpala' created the song and significantly helped plan the level's visuals.

name: Swing Swing
description: A swing-only extreme demon with a super creative name hosted by Kaza. Went unrated for a really really long time

name: Guideless Goobering
description: Colorful rainbow memory level acting as the finale in Enfur's unnamed memory trilogy. Features lots of bright and cluttered decoration and unique effects. Memorable for its tricky misaligned dual at 77%

name: Hard Machine
description: Iconic design level with unchanged gameplay originally made in 1.8 in an effort to be harder than ICE Carbon Diablo X. Infamous for its 46% "shalom" mini-ufo spam among other difficult sections.

name: DISSONANCE
description: A solo effect level created to match its dissonant song, standing out amongst other levels around its difficulty with its slow, precision-based gameplay and bizarre effects. Received a legendary rating from RobTop.

name: Goober Rage Stage
description: Medium length, Robot-only memory level with lots of trolls that have to be memorised and subsequently avoided. Well known and quite well received.

name: Crackhead Circles
description: Extremely bright and flashy rainbow circles level by AlrexX. Represents the ridiculous mumble trap-metal song with psychedelic visuals and high-energy timings. Don't do drugs

name: Zodiac
description: Legendary former top 1 that was hack verified by xander556 before being verified legitimately by Technical. Features a classic Creo song, bright but simplistic visuals, and infamous chokepoints.

name: Lotus Flower
description: Beloved gimmicky neo-design level by duo zzzgecko and StarkyTheSalad. Known for its insanely difficult to control duals and Radiohead song, as well as its washed out and laid-back visuals.

name: Scream Machine
description: 3-minute-long horror themed megacollab with great immersion and various creepy pop culture references. Features unique gameplay and decoration heavily inspired by 2.0.

name: Axinie
description: Semi-generic early 2.2 extreme using a flashy bright pink and blue color loop throughout the level. Consists of high-cps timings and multiple swing sections.

name: Judgement Knights
description: The sequel to Knights of Thunder, featuring flashy colors and an extremely intense song. Memorable for its unique colors and futuristic city-themed visuals. Often abbreviated to JKOT.

name: Widestep
description: Blue and purple design level with early 2.1 adjacent designs. Features incredibly fast-paced gamemode switches and difficult timings. Apparently one of the hardest solo-created and verified levels of its time. The Lucky Potion Has No Effects On Gameplay.

name: Lithium
description: Sleek memory-based extreme with gimmicky gameplay and classic desaturated designs. The gameplay is note-worthy for its incredible amount of learniness and unique structuring.

name: Keres
description: Extended and buffed Erebus remake with a large roster of creators. Primarily timings-based with complex and awkward gameplay throughout.

name: IRIS
description: A rapid-paced extreme collab which contains mainly vibrant blue, purple and pink coloring and a variety of eye-catching effects that capture the intensity of the level's song. The gameplay is very well-regarded despite the challenge it provides. The wave parts are considered to be the most difficult, and the level requires a high amount of CPS.

name: Cold Sweat
description: Legendary but ultimately aged effect level with an incredibly memorable spam ufo and high-cps wave part at 61%. Previously was top 7 and was one of the best top levels of its time, also being heavily memed by the community for years.

name: Frost Spirit
description: XL 1.9 styled extreme excellently capturing an icy cold environment within it's restrictions. The levels difficulty is primarily in its difficult cube and ship sections. Level name and song are from Tower Defense Simulator on Roblox.

name: Ascent
description: Short neon-green glow megacollab with flashy pulses and difficult ship and wave sections. Features an incredibly laggy realistic endscreen and a very loose forest theme.

name: Promethean
description: Medium length orange hell level with an intense metal song and a multitude of clubstep monsters. Notorously ship-carried and has a note-worthily difficult ending. Took over 115,000 attempts to verify.

name: Thinking Space
description: Former impossible level known for its extreme difficulty spike in the final third. Incredibly unique in terms of both its gameplay and decoration, featuring absolutely absurd chokepoints and a backwards-moving wave part, a clear product of early 2.0.

name: Dry Out Copyable 2
description: Very long and colorful reddit extreme memorable for its unique effects and many pop-culture and GD references. Features a general rainbow color scheme and lots of variation in its designs. The ending copies Dry Out's gameplay and turns it into memory. Unfortunately is not a copyable version of Dry Out.

name: Calculator Core
description: Classic 2019 megacollab remaking and extending TeamN2's Artificial Ideology. Extremely difficult for its time, and stays true to the original while upgrading the decoration and doubling the length. Primarily greyscale with fast-paced gameplay very reminiscent of early 2.1.

name: Renevant
description: Allegiance sequel with a darker aesthetic than its predecessor. Features flowy muscle-memory gameplay and a surprising amount of quick UFO sections, along with occasional chokepoints throughout. They also misspelled the name

name: ConClusion
description: The final and hardest level of the ConTrilogy. This level holds nothing back and utilizes memory to its fullest while including flashbacks from both ConTroller and ConFusion. The wave part is known for being very intimidating to approach and can be considered one of the most intense parts of a memory level. Now, can you escape your mind and conclude this nightmare once and for all?

name: We Are Not The Same (Solo)
description: 2-player duo collab where player 2's part is extreme and player 1's is significantly easier to the point of non-demon gameplay, hence the name. Consists of themeless and pretty low-key visuals, including a review about the mario movie at end. I don't think we're alike at all Mr. White...

name: Trotil
description: Glass themed megacollab often mistaken for a winter theme, as the difference is pretty much impossible to differentiate. Memorable for its unique decoration style and realistic endscreen. Creators are mostly unknown because the gameplay and decoration were made by participants/subscribers in Russian youtuber Ani's creator contest.

name: Instinct (Krazyman50)
description: Unofficial part of the Pokemon Go series (Valor & Mystic) and features yellow and black electric designs, remaining somewhat simplistic throughout. Memorable for its charm and inconsistent style. Generally more experimental than ItsHybrids' Instinct.

name: Sky Shredder
description: Formerly the hardest 1.9GDPS extreme. Mostly monochromatic and remains high-energy through most of the level, with difficult ship and wave parts. The name comes from a Bloons TD 6 Monkey Ace upgrade. "The bloons will wish they had never come..."

name: shimmer (amplitron)
description: Incredibly fast-paced green and pink extreme for Zeroniums 2023 creator contest. Made entirely solo and features dream-like neo-design and effect decoration with a reoccuring flower motif in the first half. Most recognizable for its learny space themed ending and atmospheric breakcore song.

name: Crimson Planet
description: Legendary former top 1 with an intense dubstep song and a loose otherworldly theme. Incredibly reminiscent of early 2.1, being started only months after 2.1's release. Includes Terron's iconic part repurposed from Yatagarasu and a modern endscreen. Took too long to get rated. Do not abbreviate on Discord (seriously).

name: DIRECTIONS
description: Unique XL level with a loose northern lights theme in the beginning, turning into a fast-paced and more modern-esque flurry of color. Memorable for the 2nd half being mirrored, making the gameplay significantly more difficult to learn. Includes a floaty progressive breakbeat song to match its unique visuals.

name: ATOMIC CANNON Mk II
description: The second installment of the ATOMIC CANNON series made a year after the prequel. The level has higher cps gameplay and expands upon the original level by including new elements such as featuring the wave and briefly the spider and robot game modes. This is also the first in the series to have additional gameplay creators.

name: Cognition
description: Iconic claustrophobic former top 3 extreme with an old fashioned song featuring Charlie Chaplin's speech from The Great Dictator. Known for its incredibly precise gameplay, monochromatic visuals and dark atmosphere.

name: Cosmic Cyclone
description: APTeam's standout project, acting as the sequel to Sonic Wave. Consists of exceedingly bright effects and flashy space-themed designs similarly to Sonic Wave Infinity. Took 4 years to be created and verified, starting in 2017.

name: qoUEO
description: Foggy neo-design-esque level memorable for its balancing, all the difficulty being in the first 26% in the form of an ultra-precise challenge-like wave part. Often referred to as "an extended challenge list level".

name: SARY NEVER CLEAR
description: Incredibly un-serious remake of the 1.6 formerly-impossible level by Optic Sunflow under the same name with altered gameplay. The level's silly text and charming early 2.1-esque decoration enabled it to get heavily memed by the community. HOW ARE YOU CLEAR MY MAP???

name: Rigel
description: Solo created and verified, space-themed Nine Circles level in tech style. The waves are constantly 4x speed, with the first one being far harder than the second. Has a very chokable ending.

name: Scrubbabingo force
description: A memory extreme which started off as a buffed version of Hexagon Force by Shubhy before being decorated and polished into a full level by Akunakun 3 years later. It showcases a wide array of quirky gameplay gimmicks such as horizontally desynced duals and a wave double click. Its decoration takes heavy inspiration from Creeper Force such as the black-and-white ending.

name: Call Me Maybe
description: A pink extreme demon with many designs reflecting the simpler, traditional side of Geometry Dash, paired with an early 2010s pop song. Its gameplay is very clicksync based and relies on player movements to sync with the song, alongside especially brutal chokepoints in the ending and the predrop.

name: ta1LSD0ll
description: Extremely short and hyper-flashy effect level with incredibly learny wave sections. Constructed to be as psychedelic and otherworldly as possible greatly assisted by 2.2 shader spam. Unique endscreen by the mysterious 2003Devin.

name: RUST
description: A solo timing-based extreme formerly in the Top 20 which became highly popular among demonlist players for its accessibility. It combines sharp movements, non-blending glow and a washed-out colour scheme to create a gritty atmosphere, hence its name. Its gameplay takes heavy inspiration from KrmaL and crohn44, and is notable for its usage of double clicks and a long stress test at the end.

name: Gloxinia
description: A mostly Korean Mega-collab started in late 2022 by  Aeidux and later finished by Solarion. Gloxinia feature early 2.1 style deco  similar to Zodiac and Esfera. The level contains flow gameplay and is an XL spanning just over 2 minutes.

name: meow hard
description: Hexagon force themed level with plenty of cats around, with very hard timing gameplay that concludes in a pretty creative wave at the ending.

name: Launchpad Labyrinth
description: A fully half-speed level based on a level in the game “Wario Land: Shake It!” with the same name. The gameplay is intended to be annoying, being similar to the game's level. The level was originally hack verified.

name: SUPERHATEMEWORLD
description: Redecorated version of a 1.9GDPS impossible level by Icedcave. Generic hell-themed extreme but memorable due to its awkward, slow and precise gameplay. The majority of the difficulty lies in the ship and ball sections. Designed intentionally to make the player hate it.

name: Shardscapes
description: Minimalist-style timing-based extreme using only the robot gamemode. Its gameplay is notable for its usage of spike corner jumps and multi-activated orbs, and its decoration combines parallax, enter/exit effects and a calm Creo song to create a dream-like atmosphere.

name: Omega Interface
description: The final entry in Platnuu's "Interface" level trilogy, featuring dense, grayscale design decoration with flashes of bright red. Contains claustrophobic gameplay that emphasizes the intensity of the song.

name: Sides Of My Mind (Solo)
description: A 2-player level that splits apart and then recombines together in the end. Visuals contain a lot of contrasting effects and feature rainbow and monochrome palette. Very first half carried for solo play.

name: Horros (Solo)
description: A 2-player level that has no consistent theme, as there is "no sense of continuity in dreams". For solo play, it is infamous for the difficulty of the first and third dual, namely because of the 2p ship control.

name: me when machine gang (Solo)
description: A 2-player memory level by akunakun that was made in one sitting, with mostly path-based memory other than the splitscreen dual. For solo play, 88-100 is a major chokepoint.

name: The Art of the Blade
description: The winner of MatMart's GD Civilization Contest thanks to its ludicrous gimmick of a randomized maze where the correct path is determined by the colour of three subtle details throughout the level. Even outside of its gimmick, the level features very unorthodox gameplay structuring and frontloaded balancing.

name: Deep Bass
description: An official sequel to Down Bass, with extremely challenging wave segments — most notably at 48% and 56% — followed by a significantly easier second half.

name: Jupiter My Favourite
description: A blue and yellow solo memory extreme with many doodle-like designs and a lot of wacky gameplay structures and gimmicks, most notably the 24% wave.

name: no jokes
description: Short and very serious level with no silliness allowed. Known for its extremely concentrated difficulty and chokepoints. Are you brave enough?

name: Bloom
description: A minimalist atmospheric XXL solo extreme that won $300 in KrmaL's Contest 3. Consists of slow paced, experimental gameplay, a terrifying learning curve, and some randomization. Its style takes great inspiration from a level of the same name by KrmaL in Soundodger+

name: LD50
description: An incredibly learny level featuring fast click patterns and complex gameplay mechanics, such as the ending where the player must avoid breaking breakable blocks so they form a bridge over a pit of saws. Starts off slow and easy, with the CPS, complexity and the difficulty increasing as the level progresses.

name: the ocean of fish
description: Solo XXL extreme containing many fish and other sea creatures made of spikes and other basic gd blocks. All interactable objects in the level are fully incorporated into the decoration and fit the level's theme. The gameplay itself is slow paced, memory heavy, contains a very complex dual, and has a tough first wave.

name: Nabil Let Go
description: A 1.9 GDPS level which fittingly uses the main level song Can't Let Go. Initially starting out as a joke level, it has gone through many buffs and nerfs during its creation and consists of several wave chokepoints and a significant focus on timings. Contains a Jeff the Killer cameo.

name: Sank
description: The hardest rated cube-only level at the time of it's release. Takes major inspiration from Dump and DsinK. Has precise orb timings, and some silly Spongebob characters near the end.

name: Asterios
description: An old dual-heavy level hosted by Exen with an underlying space theme. Was initially built to be Top 1, but was heavily nerfed to be Top 20 at the time. Currently Exen's most well-received level thanks to its satisfying dual gameplay, though it being slightly held back by its predrop and bugs is a common sentiment amongst victors.

name: Bloodlust
description: Legendary former Top 1 and remake of Bloodbath, expanding on its predecessor with a minute-long extension of fast-paced gameplay as well as many changes to existing gameplay and an overhauled visual style. Had a monumental verification taking Knobbelboy over 1.5 years, 120k attempts and 1000 hours of playtime, which for its time was completely unheard of.

name: Moving Forward
description: The sequel to Glide and crohn44's final level, made over the course of two years. Has a more artistic aesthetic than his other works, with inspiration from Knots and Subwoofer, and features striking song representation in gameplay and visuals. Its gameplay is structured very unconventionally, and thus community reception has been very mixed.

name: Jesse Pinkman
description: The first installment of the Breaking Bad Series, featuring typical fast-paced gameplay and an early 2.1 style aesthetic. It's one of Renn's more unserious projects which was built during two off-school days, and hence it momentarily became a meme in the community for placing above Bloodlust when it first came out.

name: Excruciation Chamber
description: spcreat's (nearly) all wave level inspired by UFWM's challenge level, Torture Chamber. Decoration features a changing fire/frost theme.

name: Terminux
description: The sequel to Eternal Moment, adopting a similar gameplay and visual style though more polished and modern in many ways. Its base gameplay is mostly well-received, though there are numerous ship and wave chokepoints throughout. Made almost entirely by Korean creators, though Inex is here too for some reason.

name: CITRA
description: One of the shortest rated extremes in the game at only 39 seconds. It has flashy effects and very intense fast paced gameplay based around difficult timings and tight wave sections. It is pretty popular and generally well received, even though there are quite a lot of bugs.

name: Gustavo Fring
description: The easiest level in the Breaking Bad series. Primarily focused on tight ship, cube and flowy gameplay. It has somewhat inconsistent gameplay, and a very difficult first 30%. To avoid seeming racist, Renn didn't make a caricature like in the other Breaking Bad levels.

name: Deimos (EndLevel)
description: A redecorated, buffed remake of the unnerfed version of Phobos. There is a different endscreen if you jump right before the last dual.

name: DsinK
description: A sink themed level that was originally started by crohn44, but then finished by Nightning and stanstansmansan. The gameplay is unconventional and gimmicky, with a desynced dual part and tricky timings throughout. Drop to the ending is mostly synced with the song.

name: Congregation
description: A 3 minute long timing-based level with a long, slow-paced buildup and an intense, fast-paced climax whilst also having the lowest object density of any 2.1 extreme at the time. Due to its progression-based approach and its required skillsets, it became very devisive both in deco and difficulty opinions.

name: Pootis Engage XTREME
description: 1.9 styled memory extreme leaning towards timing skillset. 14-19 and 59-100 (particularly 59-70) are often considered major chokepoints. Sequel to "Pootis Engage" by the same creator.

name: Knights of Thunder
description: Space-themed extreme created by Hangerlord known for fast paced consistent gameplay and rapid transitions highlighting wave and ship. This level was inspired by Trueffet and Cosmic Cyclone.

name: ATOMIC CANNON
description: The first installment of the ATOMIC CANNON series and Lieb's first solo level after eight years. The level is inspired by Intercept by Viprin and features fast-paced and high cps gameplay. The final ball also references 8 levels Lieb collaborated on back in the 1.9 era.

name: Fog
description: Very well received foggy level with fast muscle memory based gameplay and a simple randomiser at the end

name: Ploink
description: Short, unserious level with a rainbow colour scheme and many jokes and references sprinkled throughout. Its gameplay is somewhat learny with its difficulty primarily coming from timing-based cube sections.

name: CONNECT
description: A deeply emotional and symbolic level originally intended to be a suicide note, portraying the creator's struggle with depression and suicidal ideation. Each part has direct references to the creator's feelings and experiences, and eventual recovery.

name: the wiener
description: Goofy 2.1 pseudo-joke level that was made in a week. The level is generally not received very well, and the wave near the end is seen as one of the worst of all time. Features a two-block-long CDMusic part.

name: Ghoul
description: One of XavT's solo extremes. Features intense and learny gameplay, with entirely grayscale decoration. This was formerly the hardest AREDL monthly extreme.

name: kowareta
description: Extremely short level which to some can feel impossible. The first green orb is maybe a little bit hard. The level that birthed the reta styled levels. Inspired by Plasma Pulse Finale.

name: EGO KILLING
description: A megacollab that was originally hacked, unrated, and reverified with no gameplay changes. This level is flooded with chokepoints and is infamous for the 78% 4x speed mini wave click. Don’t expect the greatest experience.

name: Frozen Cave
description: One of the hardest art-style extremes of its time, where the player must escape a collapsing cave high up in the frozen mountains. It's gameplay is especially notable for its wide array of gimmicks and structuring quirks, such as double clicks throughout, the final wave and the iconic toggle orb ship at 64%.

name: 2 Comme le vent
description: Remake of an obscure 1.9 level '1 Comme le vent', with more two player gameplay and an incredibly hard wave at 78%. Intended for solo play.

name: Death Corridor Z
description: Robbed Top 1 verified way back in 2017 that took nearly 9 years to get rated. Features several incredibly difficult wave chokepoints and a black and white colour scheme.

name: Arctic Lights
description: A notoriously difficult nine circles level that was the top 1 hardest nine circles level when it was verified. This was a remake to Hyper Paracosm and is themed after the color pale blue. For a long time, this was known as the list demon many would beat first, like Cobwebs and Cognition. Best known for its awkward wave gameplay and some difficult chokepoints, especially at 36-48% and 58-69%.

name: PROTON LAUNCHER
description: An ATOMIC CANNON styled level which started out as an entry for Lieb's ATOMIC CANNON Style Gameplay Creator Contest. Just like the level it is based on, it features very fast-paced high CPS gameplay with a heavy lean towards timings and a very nerve-wracking ending.

name: ROCKY ROAD
description: Fast paced, well-received timing extreme created by arclight & cdyzz.

name: Plasma Pulse Finale
description: The finale of the Plasma Pulse series and a former Top 1, making it by far the hardest. It takes everything you’ve learned from the previous levels with various precise timings (some may be chokepoints), a complicated robot + cube dual, and a nerve-wracking ending that contains robot micro clicks. This level truly tests your raw skill to its fullest.

name: Spectrum Cyclone
description: A fast-paced level with a lot of asymmetrical duals and sync-based click patterns, taking nearly 2 years to build and verify. Very well-received by the community with many deeming it as one of the most fun extreme demons in the game to this day.

name: Specthys (Solo)
description: Solo 2p level made and verified by gBen, themed around astronomy with a mostly yellow scheme. Built with the intention to be played solo, and features a lot of ship control.

name: Alpha
description: A traditional-styled level which has faced over 6 years of on-and-off building and countless buffs, nerfs and rebalances. Despite everything it went through it managed to become highly enjoyed amongst its victors. Very timing-based with the main highlight being the 39% dual, though it has several short bursts of more skill-based gameplay throughout.

name: Shutdown
description: This level has a hot/cold theme and is based around a map from Flood Escape 2. Has a brutal ship/wave at the drop and a difficult swing-copter near the middle of the level. There is also a memory part based on a previous level by OliSW, Ulon.

name: Calamity
description: Formerly the second hardest 1.9 GDPS level. The gameplay is skill based and it's difficulty is carried by the two wave sections, as well as the hard dual at the end. The level was updated by Royen to be more forgiving.

name: Cersia Madness
description: 1.9 extreme using Stereo Madness with an incredibly learny dual at the drop and very skill based gameplay in the ending and predrop. It was the least enjoyed extreme on EDEL before it was raised after a mass group completion by around 20 people. It has many skips that make the level significantly more doable.

name: Piano Woman
description: The sequel to Piano Man. A true solo, with pukklez making the song, the level, and being the verifier. Every input is 2.5 frames on 60fps.

name: WORSTBRAINMAP
description: Quirky silly memory extreme with very gimmicky parts, such as a The Art Of The Blade styled randomised wave maze, a ship part with bouncing orbs, a cutscene where you have to click to the music, and a DIRECTIONS esque slope cube. Are you a thinker?

name: Iris of a Puppeteer
description: A dark and slower paced level featuring some very tight gameplay, with a focus on waves. More learny than expected, partly due to jank, and also features a blind frame perfect at 49%.

name: Dance of the Violins
description: An old megacollab that was only recently completed and verified, still containing parts from old creators like Funnygame and Cyclic. The decoration is in the design style popular at the time, and the gameplay is infamous especially for the ridiculously hard wave by Cyclic at 14%.

name: SisuSonic
description: Finnish megacollab inspired by the sonic series, started in 2018 and finished in 2024. The hardest but also possibly the most obscure sonic series spinoff.

name: ConTroller
description: DreamZone's first solo level and the first of the ConTrilogy. This factory themed level contains heavy amounts of memory with tons of fakes similar to some levels from the 1.9 era. The factory heats up during the drop and becomes very dynamic, so try not to get too controlled with the level's fast movements.

name: Golden Club
description: A solo extreme demon made over 1.5 years, and the last level made by Zenmuron before he stepped down from the game. The level involves the player entering and exiting a golden factory.

name: Molten Gear
description: A continuation of Fusion Z. This level underwent major changes after Manix648 requested its cancellation, causing several creators to withdraw. Knobbelboy then hosted a creator contest to fill all the gaps of the older parts which couldn’t be used, hence the vast difference in quality between many parts.

name: Hyper Paracosm
description: An early 2.1 collab between ViRuZ and TheRealSneaky. It's a Nine Circles level with purple and blue theming. It's known for its chokepoint heavy and awkward gameplay, especially at the beginning of the drop. Also contains a difficult ending.

name: Collect All Pets
description: A design level by Rynoxious and Alrexx, named after the Roblox game of the same name. The gameplay is timing-based, without focusing on a specific gamemode.

name: ADOMANIA
description: Well received sequel to Ellipsism with a satisfying click pattern and very bright drop sections. The waves in the first drop are notably unbalanced, and the last click is a major chokepoint. Despite this, the level remains very well received.

name: Time Extreme
description: The second part of Vortrox's superbuffed Robtop levels series. The level mainly follows the layout of Time Machine with much harder gameplay. Similarly to Stereo Extremeness, it also contains a difficult robot part in the second half.

name: Wasureta
description: A purple "reta-styled" level recognisable for its flow gameplay style, difficult wave sections and extremely high-CPS click patterns. Its emphasis on muscle memory has made it relatively accessible for its difficulty and hence its popularity among extreme demon players.

name: Cybernetic Crescent
description: The third and final level of the Tech Trilogy. It bears a more modern spin to its decoration compared to its two predecessors, which is especially apparent in how creating styles have evolved since the trilogy's last instalment and a much newer Creo song. Its middle third is especially difficult, with click-pattern-based ship sections bearing the forefront of its difficulty.

name: Sink
description: A purple and orange extreme demon combining modern creating techniques and ideas with 1.9 editor limitations in the 1.9GDPS. Its gameplay mostly revolves around clicksync with the first two sections bearing a big chunk of the level's difficulty.

name: FIFTY THREE
description: A fast-paced, visually simple level, where the drop's gameplay is in mirror mode and cycles through all the gamemodes in chronological order (though skipping the robot). Due to a lot of effort put into balancing, it has become very enjoyed amongst its victors. One of the two Vlacc levels starting out as "layout53", the other being Cloud Nine.

name: ASHES
description: A level reflecting the creator’s struggle with mental health. Starts off extremely difficult and becomes easier as it progresses. Features an exceptionally high cps.

name: Necromancer
description: A superbuffed remake of Necropolis that has very similar structuring and color scheme to the original. The gameplay has received criticism for its forced difficulty.

name: Rage
description: A high-CPS level with 7 sections, one for each gamemode. The colour scheme for each section is based on the gamemode’s portal.

name: Sonic Wave Rebirth
description: 2.0 remake of Sonic Wave with near identical gameplay and effects that have aged to varying degrees. Despite being extremely similar it is notably less enjoyable on average.

name: Sonic Wave
description: One of the most famous extreme demons of all time, with a rich history and many remakes. An iconic blue circles level with three separate wave parts and a hard ball part in the predrop. Member of the first RGB trilogy.

name: Icotact
description: A 48 second level started by ozpectro and finished by Icedcave and Nobody. It features a lot of fast paced gameplay and ends in an infamously difficult wave.

name: LUMINA
description: A timing-based level which takes heavy inspiration from Congregation, with similar progression style, structuring, and the same gamemodes. However, this level is more bright and much more fast-paced. Initially very first-half heavy before getting a nerfdate which made it more balanced.

name: In The Generator
description: A solo extreme made in the 2.0GDPS. Has an upbeat electricity/factory theme with a very faithful 2.0 aesthetic. The gameplay is mainly skill based, with some very tight precision ship sections.

name: Yatagarasu
description: One of the most iconic 2.0 megacollabs, and one of the most memorable Top 1s of all time despite only dethroning Sonic Wave for a short time.  Contains 3 minutes of stressful gameplay from start to finish, including an incredibly difficult predrop, and chokepoints throughout, including Michigun's famous "bird click". Member of the first RGB trilogy.

name: The Fog
description: An atmospheric megacollab where the player traverses through ancient ruins whilst shrouded by dense fog. The gameplay is cube only and is heavily timing-based without using any spikes. It progressively gets more fast-paced and colourful until the ending where the sun breaks through and the fog clears as you leave the ruins behind.

name: Infinite Iniquity
description: Nearly 4-minute long sequel to Twisted Tranquility. It shares some similarities such as the beginning and the “Jetpack Mode” cube. The level is fast-paced throughout, testing the player’s nerve control.

name: Devotion
description: This 2.1 extreme is the second level in the Rededication Series, which is inspired by Nikrodox's Dedication series. This level has been in the works since 2018, initially being known as "Paradox".

name: Kuzureta
description: A "reta-style" megacollab using Yoru ni Kakeru by YOASOBI that has a notably lower CPS than its predecessors. Features a solid amount of clicksync, numerous wave chokepoints, and two blind transitions.

name: Twisted Titanium
description: Third and final level of the Tempered Steel trilogy. Verified by spcreat. (Video set as verification is a showcase)

name: Edge of the Blade
description: Vaguely themed around the Legend of Zelda game series. Features all-around gameplay with balancing skewed towards the first half, along with a handful of difficult waves and some awkward gameplay throughout.

name: xo
description: A simplistic, 1.0 style KrmaL level with minimalistic effects. Known for it's first half difficulty, as well as the infamous 3% dash orb, and some bad sync. Because of the minimalistic decoration, this level sparked some controversy over whether it deserves to be rated.

name: GONER
description: Nightning's magnum opus, serving as a collection of his thoughts and feelings during his creation process and taking inspiration from the "Blurryface" album by twenty one pilots. The decoration is entirely red, black and white, and the level builds up to a near-incomprehensible yet incredibly cathartic wave section at the song's climax. The only extreme to have its own ARG.

name: Nunri
description: Szilu's hardest solo level, a purple design level which is well balanced and uses most gamemodes.

name: Loochiverse
description: Made for the MatMart Civilization event with a bossfight against one of the creators (EpicLooch) made through image2gd, although he can't actually damage you. Got a nerfdate later on to make it more timing based and to fix the balancing issues while still keeping it predrop heavy.

name: Sunset Sandstorm
description: Iconic crohn44 extreme which takes heavy inspiration from Black Blizzard. Known for the infamous click pattern at the beginning of the level.

name: Erebus (BoldStep)
description: Member of the RGB, themed around radioactivity and having some of the most difficult raw gameplay for its time. Its gameplay is very skill-based with many difficult ship and wave sections sprinkled throughout such as the iconic 85% slope corridor, alongside a distinctive memory cube maze right before the drop.

name: Echoes of Neon
description: A remake of Forsaken Neon and MinAY's proudest megacollab project. It has the original level's colour scheme, slope-oriented structures, large orb clusters and speed portals, while overhauling the decoration with modern tech-based designs and an extension which nearly doubles its length.

name: Dark Flare
description: The final and hardest level by Lightning Collab Team, this megacollaboration has no defined decorative theme. It is best known for the part by Dotory that was significantly buffed by the verifier, Plusxenon.

name: Ddiamond
description: Colourful wave-based demon featuring Lfritz' trademark rainbow decoration and tight wave corridor gameplay similar to Diamond Blade, as well as some timings and ship gameplay.

name: Timor
description: A horror-themed level with gameplay by Colorbolt and decoration by Maxie25. It started as a top 1 layout which was later nerfed to a top 30 during its verification process. The gameplay is very brief and wave-focused.

name: Ascension
description: A four minute long rainbow level to the tune of Abracadavre from Call of Duty: Black Ops Zombies. A passion project seven years in the making with detailed rainbow designs, interesting line art, and hard gameplay throughout. One of the rawest extremes in the game.

name: Glory
description: A Christian megacollab hosted by Entique, intended to remind everyone of God's love and glory. The level itself is a purple glow-flow with an upbeat Colbreakz song.

name: Jamboree
description: Extremon which takes heavy inspiration from Spectrum Cyclone. Like its predecessor, it features learny asymmetrical dual gameplay and a very hard wave at the end.

name: Devil Vortex
description: A level that came from a denied part for Yatagarasu, which Rustam expanded on to make a full level. The decoration uses a hell theme with recognizable artworks. ToshDeluxe superbuffed and hack-verified the level, which later became one of the most infamous hacker exposals.

name: Dole Damos
description: Quirky Jeyzor level known for its unique designs and fancy transitions. The gameplay is entirely made of duals besides one transition.

name: Factory Realm X
description: Very old 2.1 grayscale level with learny duals and a bossfight (with some inconsistent layering). Generally well-received despite its age.

name: DubKore X
description: A remake of DubCore by MaJackO and Crack, this level follows the conventions of MaJackO’s “Core” series, featuring 1.9-style decoration and primarily slow paced gameplay.

name: Next Stage
description: Zipixbox and TMco collab that is the successor to Heavens Door. Inspired by the Stage 4 boss from I Wanna Kill The Kamilia 3, and a 2.0 level of the same name. Features flashbacks to various older levels in reference to the IWKTK3 bossfight, as well as fully randomized bullet hell styled parts.

name: ConFusion
description: The second and easiest level of the ConTrilogy. Despite not being as fast as the original, this fusion laboratory still contains a hectic amount of movements. With the amount of flashing pulses and fakes featured, your learning experience may be too confusing to handle.

name: Retention Rush
description: Very purple memory extreme that mostly consists of fake structures, orbs and portals. The ending requires hitting certain coins after the "listen closely" text. Otherwise, you get geometrically dominated.

name: FIREPOWER (Sillow)
description: One of the longest nine circles extremes with a highly diverse skillset range. Has a high quality orange design style with a lot of character, featuring many creatures and monsters, and even has a snake that you fly through. Gets gradually harder and faster paced as the level progresses, and features hard asymmetrical duals.

name: 0 Techno
description: Cherryteam collab hosted by MinAY, 'designed to make your eyes bleed'. Verification video is a placeholder showcase

name: lacy
description: Calm pink XL megacollab to the tune of Lacy by Olivia Rodrigo. Has an asymmetrical dual in the middle where your icon can land on your ship.

name: POW
description: Super spammy level that is the sequel to WOW

name: Death
description: A nine minute long emotionally powerful level that took over six years to complete. The first third is noticeably harder, but there is still tough gameplay very far into the level. A tribute to TSGuy, may he rest in peace.

name: Apollo 11
description: The finale to Nasgubb's Space Trilogy that took over two years to create. A very long moon themed extreme with mostly simple consistency based gameplay, though it does feature a few memory/gimmicky sections. Known for being very stressful but enjoyable.

name: TheCinnyBun
description: A joke level Renn241 created for the player Cinny. The gameplay is made up of super fast flow gameplay and extremely difficult wave sections.

name: Digital Descent
description: A former Top 1 which was originally easier than its predecessor until it got a buffdate. The decoration follows a similar theme to Artifical Ascent with 2.1 elements sprinkled throughout, and its difficulty notably spikes in the middle third of the level.

name: Stalemate Redux
description: A remake of the insane demon Stalemate, which was falsely placed at #1 as the verifier insisted upon it being harder than Yatagarasu. The level features early 2.1 decoration, often dark and obscuring, with the 76–100% section being picked through a creator contest.

name: ZAPHKIEL
description: A former top level from early 2.1 notable for its extremely difficult wave parts, an iconic and climactic lava-themed section in the second half, and overall its awkward yet oddly satisfying gameplay. Initially hack-verified by Noctafly.

name: Omicron
description: Unofficial greek letter mega collab hosted by Team Proxima. Has a cool minigame at the end for a coin (Bugfix: 120902367)

name: Xronier
description: A short, red and blue, pure solo extreme made and verified by now-music artist JinoBeats. Matching the song's chaotic, high-tempo energy its visuals are extremely flashy and its gameplay features many high-CPS bursts and is fast-paced all around. Very well-received amongst victors for getting extremely consistent after some practice, in part thanks to the update it received in 2.2.

name: Video
description: An 8 minute long level themed around MaxxoRMeN's experiences with internet addiction. It has a randomised minigame section where you have to look out for Mr. Wireless and is accompanied by nearly a minute of difficult fast-paced gameplay in the intro and a high-energy drop with a learny dual nearly 6 minutes in. Extremely well-received for its progression and song representation.

name: RGB
description: A solo 2.1 extreme demon made by SleyGD themed after the colors red, green, and blue. The level contains many different flashy background effects and a interesting mix of learny and skill-based gameplay. The level is notorious for its very laggy full detail mode and very difficult asymmetrical dual from 76-100.

name: Adelucid
description: A gimmicky and fast-paced Extreme Demon utilizing Passable blocks, orb-based gameplay and uniquely ethereal decoration. Currently the hardest "full solo" level in the game, with the creator having finished the entire song, gameplay, decoration and verification by himself.

name: lovel
description: Love themed memory & design level with a mostly hot pink colour scheme. The hardest part is the drop with an incredibly difficult mini wave dual. It was made for valentines day, but was a few days late with its release.

name: Tempered Steel
description: Originally an impossible top 1, which was later nerfed to top 50 after being hosted by ItsHybrid. It is the first level in the Metal trilogy. The decoration is themed around steel and heat, with a gray and yellow theme.

name: Killbot
description: A memory level heavily inspired by the 1.9 level “Chaotic Machine.” The level is themed around a virus installing itself on the player's computer, visually obstructing the gameplay through flashes on the screen on top of a lot of fake routes. Also has an infamous UFO chokepoint at the end.

name: vaenstep
description: A typical Renn241 extreme named after the player vaen, who verified and published the level. It is especially skill-based with the slow yet nerve-wracking 72% section being a main highlight. Its decoration uses a diverse colour palette and becomes a colourful mess during the drop despite the level's overall simplicity.

name: The Moon Below
description: The first of two levels in the Beneath - Upward: Perfume EP by Onvil, preceding The Garden Above. It is the harder of the two by far, featuring a cube section with near-invisible timings and a ship section demanding a lot of ship control, separated by a 20 second-long dash orb. The level uses glow objects, cold colours, and is entirely in slow speed to create a tranquil, melancholic atmosphere.

name: find the answer
description: A solo extreme demon made by qalli, which uses a follow trigger setup to make new and intuitive dual gameplay that has never been seen before!!!

name: Artificial Ascent
description: An extremely popular former top 1 megacollab made in 2.0, initially hosted by LmAnubis and finished by Viprin. The level contains a variety of fast-paced gameplay parts made by 18 different creators, spanning almost 2 and a half minutes. It went on to have multiple remakes and sequels, becoming the first installment of the Technology Trilogy.

name: Edelweiss
description: Ridiculously high CPS and fast-paced mountain themed extreme demon with super learny and middle-carried gameplay and a complex click pattern. Many of the hitboxes are extra lenient, and despite some people experiencing bugs and chokepoints, the level is very well received and highly enjoyed, and many people do it as a hardest. Pronounced ay-dull-vice.

name: TURN THE LIGHTS OFF
description: Zeronium CC entry that uses a Tally Hall song about puberty. The predrop and intermission are shrouded in darkness to match the name (someone turned the lights off). Said parts are also the hardest.

name: Stereo Extremeness
description: A popular superbuff of Stereo Madness with gameplay made by Vortrox. The level contains very precise timing gameplay, mostly following the layout of Stereo Madness with a difficult robot section in the second half. Was initially made for a youtube video and was later decorated following the layout's initial success.

name: Low Death
description: A superbuffed remake of High Life by Migueword featuring KrmaL's signature unorthodox gameplay with difficult wave and ship sections. There are many easter eggs and ancient memes scattered around the level.

name: Bete Noire
description: The unofficial sequel to the Nine Circles level Fear Me, this extremon is space/electric themed and is very reminiscent of levels like Cosmic Cyclone. Has an endscreen which lasts over 30% of the level.

name: Belloq
description: A desert-themed level based on Pennutoh’s “Spicy Layout #10.” The gameplay emphasizes clicksync, with decoration themed around Egyptian culture.

name: Diamond Disco
description: A pure solo extreme one year in the making, notable for its high-CPS clicksync gameplay, an incredibly mesmerising dual and its colour bass-complextro hybrid song. It focuses heavily on song representation, and uses editor particles, bright flashes, screen effects, complementary colours and player movements to create the vibe of a loud EDM disco party.

Well known old level that was abandoned after one of its creators quit the game in 2016, but was revived and verified seemingly out of nowhere in 2020. The gameplay is generally well received, but it's very first half heavy. It's well known for its effecty background, with simple silhouette structures. The level is exactly 2 minutes and 11 seconds long, matching the level's name.

name: Funnyname Funnygame
description: A three minute long 1.9 effect style solo level that took over two years to create, with parts very reminiscent of Lightwave. The difficulty increases as the level progresses, and the second half features some beautifully janky duals and the infamous Lightwave wave.

name: Missing Benefits
description: The first and only megacollaboration by Team Caliber, with gimmicky gameplay and dark, greyscale decoration. Known for the last 20%, which is significantly harder than the rest of the level.

name: Agony
description: A Congregation-inspired solo extreme which, as the name suggests, is themed around agony with its bright flashes, flesh-like designs and an appropriately-chosen song. Its decoration is made mostly out of the 2.2 pixel objects, and hence it has a surprisingly low object count despite its level of detail and its length.

name: Quantum Processing
description: A superbuffed remake of the main level Blast Processing, with gameplay by Riot and Hinds and a short section by Zobros.

name: Black Blizzard
description: Former top 10 solo level by KrmaL. Initially this level was harder and being verified in secret, but after the level was leaked through a hack, KrmaL verified and released a nerfed version. This level got Creo well known within the community. The gameplay is gimmicky and extremely predrop carried.

name: Neurostasis
description: The long standing former top 1 level on the enjoyments list. This level has gimmicky memory gameplay throughout, and most of it is in the ship gamemode with a lot of orbs.

name: Ethereal Artifice
description: Generally well recieved 2.1 megacollab with a red and green color scheme. It's a fast-paced XL level spanning just over two minutes in length.

name: Glowy
description: A level with gameplay by Sunnett and decoration by Rob Buck. The decoration gradually becomes more object-heavy throughout the level, making it harder to see as the level goes on. The level was meant to mock other glow-heavy levels at the time.

name: SOLEIL
description: Well received solo design-art-effect level centered around the sun, moon, and stars. Known to progressively get more difficult, and also have chokepoints in the break part at 44%.

name: REDEEMER
description: A 7 minute long extreme where giant humanoid spirits are released from their tombs, destroying the earth as they ascend. Widely praised for its atmosphere, storytelling, and song representation. The gameplay is very gimmicky, and features a very hard central section and an asymmetrical ship dual at the end.

name: BuTiTi III
description: Jonathan's almost cancelled threequel to his infamous Butiti series. The level is XXL with second half carried balancing, learny gameplay and interesting coin routes.

name: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB
description: 2.1 Megacollab hosted by Roimousti. It is the sequel to another Roimousti level called "AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA". Infamous for the 88% blue pad, and is generally rather negatively perceived.

name: Bass Cave
description: This level was originally a hard demon, which then got superbuffed and hack-verified. The level later got another update and a legitimate verification by nSwish. Known for it's chokepoint heavy gameplay and the goofy looking demon face at 14%.

name: Visurient
description: Really old 2.1 level that was made for their friend Visurient. Took a few years to get rated.

name: Magnificent Gaps
description: A magnificent remake of Impossible Gaps with a lot of finicky gimmicks and a short 2 player part. The level is fully ship gamemode with simple cute designs and visuals. The lord of chokepoints and raw skill gameplay, johnnwaogus magnificent masterpiece

name: The Sulphur Sea
description: A very fast-paced, mini-only solo extreme themed around the Sulphurous Sea from Terraria's Calamity Mod, with a blocky visual style and progressing through the layers of the Abyss, ending off on a randomised bossfight against the Primordial Wyrm. Praised in the community for its well-flowing gameplay and stunning level of detail.

name: Bordon Factory
description: Minimalistic factory themed level that uses gameplay to its fullest potential. Contains multiple gimmicky sections and a randomised bossfight.

name: Hatred
description: 2.0 demon made up of extremely difficult timing gameplay, an infamous ship gravity portal corridor at 70%, and to top it all off, a memory based mini cube section towards the end. Often draws comparisons to Acropolis due to its similar gameplay and theming.

name: Voyager 1
description: A 2.0 styled megacollab one year in the making, encapsulating a variety of decoration styles and gameplay tropes from that era. Throughout its 2 and a half minute runtime it features raw decoration, moving objects and bizarre duals galore, and paired with an old Bossfight song makes for a charming and authentic experience.

name: Auditory Breaker
description: Nerfed remake of Sonic Wave made by Lazerblitz and Manix featuring early 2.1 tech style decoration.

name: lodin da fish washer
description: 🐟 A surreal modern style cube/robot timing level with very little spike usage. Features a unique and particularly hard timing (known locally as a 'fish washer') that is repeated multiple times, as well as lots more unusual timing gameplay. 🐟

name: Worse Trip
description: Loltad level with very unique deco, paired with very unique (and fun) gameplay.

name: SubSonic
description: The 4th official entry of the sonic series, and the hardest one. It was generally considered anticlimactic when it first came out, but nowadays it's gameplay is considered balanced and consistent throughout the entire level. Features an intimidating dual by Jeyzor at the ending.

name: Dolos
description: 2.1 click sync megacollab, known for its low CPS. Much of the level is a dual, and the ending is difficult and nerve-wracking. Named after the Greek god of deception.

name: Glitched Memories
description: A early 2.1 mega-collab semi-memory level that remixes many clubstep inspired levels. (ie Clubstep, Ice Carbon Diablo X, Tartarus, Red World, 9Step, and Clubstep Nightmares)

name: MONSTROSITY
description: A level that was hack-verified, and as such has a lot of chokepoints, such as many parts in the pre-drop and the section around 42%. The gameplay is timing-based and gimmicky, with a repeated ship control section in the last 25%.

name: Supreme Demon Mix
description: A reimagining of Zobros' "Ultimate Demon Mix", often containing fast-paced gameplay requiring a variety of skillsets. Each part of the level is styled after one of 23 different demons.

name: Kinetic Bypass
description: Kinetic Bypass is a fast, skill-based 2.1 design level by Sminx and verified by Luqualizer. Despite its shorter length, it uses a wide variety of gameplay styles; featuring a slow predrop, wave, robot, a long dual, and flow gameplay.

name: milo
description: A Classic styled xSlendy level seemingly inspired by Saul Goodman. Startpos: 120275555

name: Athanatos
description: Hack-verified level from 2016 which includes very difficult wave chokepoints, including an infamously difficult dual wave at 70%. It is one of the many hell-themed top 1s, surpassing Sakupen Hell when it was placed.

name: Gentlemens Clique (Solo)
description: A 2-player level, a part of the gang trilogy. The level's name is meant to be a British version of Based Gang. Has a key you can collect to turn the bossfight icons into the Bass Cave demon.
